prerequisites: A running Couchbase Server cluster (self-managed or Couchbase Capella) reachable from the connector.
The connection_string for the cluster, using the couchbase:// (unencrypted) or couchbases:// (TLS) scheme followed by one or more node hostnames or IP addresses.
The name of the bucket you want to read data from.
A Couchbase local-domain user (username and password) with a role that grants read access to the target bucket, such as Data Reader (key-value reads) or Query Select (SQL++ SELECT statements).
Optionally, a start_date to bound incremental syncs.

authMethods.name: Username and password (RBAC)
authMethods.summary: Couchbase Server authenticates clients with a username and password assigned to a local-domain user. The connector supplies the configured username and the secret password; the user must hold a role granting read access to the bucket (for example Data Reader for key-value retrieval or Query Select for SQL++ SELECT queries). This is the password secret field in the connector config.

setupSteps.title: Create a Couchbase user with read access
setupSteps.body: In the Couchbase Web Console, go to Security > Users & Groups and open the Add User tab. Enter a Username and Password, then under Roles expand a category and assign a read role scoped to your bucket, such as Data Reader (key-value reads) or Query Select (SQL++ SELECT). Roles can apply to all or a subset of buckets, scopes, and collections. Click Save. You can also create the user with couchbase-cli user-manage --set --rbac-username <username> --rbac-password <password> --roles <role[bucket]> --auth-domain local.

setupSteps.title: Determine the connection string
setupSteps.body: Build the connection_string from your cluster node hostnames or IP addresses, prefixed with couchbase:// for an unencrypted connection or couchbases:// for a TLS-encrypted connection (for example couchbase://127.0.0.1 or couchbases://nodeA.example.com,nodeB.example.com). The string is a comma-delimited list of seed nodes; credentials are passed separately, not embedded in the string.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the connection_string, the bucket to read from, the username, and the password (secret) of the user you created. Optionally set start_date to limit the range of records synced. The connector connects to the cluster with these credentials to extract data from the specified bucket.

sources.title: Couchbase Server: Roles (Data Reader, Query Select)
